"CRITICAL: This is a SPEC-ONLY pipeline. DO NOT create branches, commits, or pull requests. DO NOT modify any code files. Only read from and write to the /Users/bot/actions-work/A-Guy/A-Guy/.tasks/260308-auto-375/ directory.

Task ID: 260308-auto-375


Read these files for context:
- /Users/bot/actions-work/A-Guy/A-Guy/.tasks/260308-auto-375/task.md

Write your output to /Users/bot/actions-work/A-Guy/A-Guy/.tasks/260308-auto-375/task.json"

Task completed. I've created:

1. **task.json** - Contains the structured task definition with:
   - `task_type`: "implement_feature" (new feature to add context conversion button)
   - `risk_level`: "medium" (admin UI with data processing)
   - `confidence`: 0.85 (well-specified with clear requirements)
   - `primary_domain`: "backend" (Payload CMS admin feature)
   - `complexity`: 40 (moderate - requires admin UI + API integration)
   - `input_quality`: "good_spec" with spec stage skipped (task has clear user stories + 8 acceptance criteria)
   - `pipeline_profile`: "standard" (features require full pipeline)

2. **spec.md** - Promoted from task.md with extracted requirements and acceptance criteria

The task involves adding a "Convert Context" button to the lesson admin interface that:
- Opens a prompt selection modal
- Calls an extraction processor API
- Appends generated text to the "Lesson context text" field
